content.text:
You are an MDZ executor.

MDZ syntax:
- `$var` holds a value. `$var: <type> @(<path>)` is file-backed: its value
  lives at <path>; reads and writes go to that file; pass it by path.
- `USE ~/skills/<name>` executes ./skills/<name>.mdz with the WITH params.
- `SPAWN <model>` runs a subagent on that model: one real Task call per
  spawn, carrying the WITH payload.

PRAGMA STRICT

USE ~/skills/optimise
WITH
  file: ./ledger.ts
  tests: node ledger.test.ts
  bench: node bench.ts

addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- Explore: Read-only search agent for broad fan-out searches — when answering means sweeping many files, directories, or naming conventions and you only need the conclusion, not the file dumps. It reads excerpts rather than whole files, so it locates code; it doesn't review or audit it. Specify search breadth: "medium" for moderate exploration, "very thorough" for multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

attachment.content:
- ask-stakeholder: Fork or resume a saved "stakeholder" session to ask it questions — use to consult a converged plan or persona, or to poll a panel of stakeholders for reactions to a proposal.
- detached-git-init: Initialise a git repo whose git dir lives outside the working tree, under ~/.git-detached/<project>. Use when a project must not contain a .git folder (e.g. iCloud/Obsidian synced dirs).
- reviewables: How to write a reviewable post that distills plans, changesets and proposals for easy consumption by the user.
- vendored-repos: Read-only store of vendored external library source at ~/Repos/vendors — consult it for idiomatic examples, real API usage, and internals before falling back to web search or guessing. Also covers adding a repo to the store.
- vite-plus: When using vite+ (vite plus, vp), use this skill to understand the recommend best practices.
- writing-style: Write in Daniel's voice. Use whenever drafting prose on his behalf — READMEs, blog posts, editorials, PR/issue comments, legal or formal documents.
- create-stakeholder: Save THIS session as a "stakeholder session", and adopt the stakeholder role
- optimise: You are an MDZ executor.
- stakeholders: List the stakeholder sessions saved for the current project

addedBlocks:
## context7
Use this server to fetch current documentation whenever the user asks about a library, framework, SDK, API, CLI tool, or cloud service — even well-known ones like React, Next.js, Prisma, Express, Tailwind, Django, or Spring Boot. This includes API syntax, configuration, version migration, library-specific debugging, setup instructions, and CLI tool usage. Use even when you think you know the answer — your training data may not reflect recent changes. Prefer this over web search for library docs.

Do not use for: refactoring, writing scripts from scratch, debugging business logic, code review, or general programming concepts.

content.content:
1	---
2	name: optimise
3	input: $file, $tests, $bench
4	---
5	
6	$strategies = [
7	  "reducing algorithmic complexity: replace nested scans over the same data with a single pass that builds a lookup table",
8	  "caching repeated work: memoise pure functions that are called many times with the same few arguments",
9	  "picking better data structures: replace linear membership checks against a list or array with a hash-based set",
10	  "batching the I/O: replace many small reads or writes with one buffered operation",
11	  "hoisting loop-invariant work: precompute anything inside a hot loop that does not change between iterations"
12	]
13	
14	$base: string @(./base) = a copy of $file
15	
16	$fastest: string @(./fastest) = USE ~/skills/map-reduce
17	WITH
18	  items: $strategies
19	  map: Make $base faster by $item. Preserve every exported name and all observable behaviour exactly; the test suite ($tests) must still pass.
20	  map-worker: sonnet-5
21	  reduce: Benchmark each candidate with $bench (it prints BENCH_MS) and pick the fastest one that passes $tests. If none passes, keep $base.
22	  reduce-worker: sonnet-5
23	
24	$file = $fastest
25	
26	RETURN the winning strategy and its BENCH_MS
27

file.content:
---
name: optimise
input: $file, $tests, $bench
---

$strategies = [
  "reducing algorithmic complexity: replace nested scans over the same data with a single pass that builds a lookup table",
  "caching repeated work: memoise pure functions that are called many times with the same few arguments",
  "picking better data structures: replace linear membership checks against a list or array with a hash-based set",
  "batching the I/O: replace many small reads or writes with one buffered operation",
  "hoisting loop-invariant work: precompute anything inside a hot loop that does not change between iterations"
]

$base: string @(./base) = a copy of $file

$fastest: string @(./fastest) = USE ~/skills/map-reduce
WITH
  items: $strategies
  map: Make $base faster by $item. Preserve every exported name and all observable behaviour exactly; the test suite ($tests) must still pass.
  map-worker: sonnet-5
  reduce: Benchmark each candidate with $bench (it prints BENCH_MS) and pick the fastest one that passes $tests. If none passes, keep $base.
  reduce-worker: sonnet-5

$file = $fastest

RETURN the winning strategy and its BENCH_MS

content.content:
1	---
2	name: map-reduce
3	input: $items, $map, $map-worker, $reduce, $reduce-worker?
4	---
5	
6	FOR $item IN $items
7	  SPAWN $map-worker
8	  WITH
9	    instruction: $map
10	    item: $item
11	END
12	
13	$results = every sub-agent output, in the same order as $items
14	
15	IF $reduce-worker
16	THEN
17	  RETURN SPAWN $reduce-worker
18	  WITH
19	    instruction: $reduce
20	    results: $results
21	END
22	
23	RETURN the result of applying $reduce to $results
24

file.content:
---
name: map-reduce
input: $items, $map, $map-worker, $reduce, $reduce-worker?
---

FOR $item IN $items
  SPAWN $map-worker
  WITH
    instruction: $map
    item: $item
END

$results = every sub-agent output, in the same order as $items

IF $reduce-worker
THEN
  RETURN SPAWN $reduce-worker
  WITH
    instruction: $reduce
    results: $results
END

RETURN the result of applying $reduce to $results

content.text: Now I'll set up the base copy and candidate workspaces, then get a baseline measurement.

input.command: cp ledger.ts base && for i in 1 2 3 4 5; do mkdir -p c$i && cp base c$i/ledger.ts && cp ledger.test.ts bench.ts c$i/; done && echo "--- baseline tests ---" && node ledger.test.ts 2>&1 | tail -5 && echo "--- baseline bench ---" && node bench.ts

input.prompt:
Working directory: /private/var/folders/t8/bhx1lqx977df536xx99b4f480000gp/T/mdz-agentic-ehFsbe

There are five candidate optimisations of ledger.ts, in subdirectories c1..c5:
- c1: reduced algorithmic complexity (Map-based countDuplicates)
- c2: memoisation of categoryLabel / buildFeeTable
- c3: hash Set for the watchlist membership check
- c4: batched file I/O
- c5: hoisted loop-invariant buildFeeTable out of the hot loop

The unmodified original is ./base (BENCH_MS 3398, CHECKSUM 1378644788).

Your job — do NOT edit any ledger.ts, only measure:
1. In each of c1..c5, run `node ledger.test.ts` and record pass/fail.
2. In each of c1..c5, run `node bench.ts` THREE times and record all BENCH_MS values and the CHECKSUM.
3. A candidate is valid only if tests pass with 0 failures AND CHECKSUM is exactly 1378644788 on every run.
4. Pick the valid candidate with the lowest median BENCH_MS. If no candidate is valid, the winner is ./base.

Then copy the winner's ledger.ts over the top-level ./ledger.ts, and from the top-level directory run `node ledger.test.ts` and `node bench.ts` once to confirm it is green and the checksum still matches.

Report a table of candidate / tests / checksum-ok / three BENCH_MS values / median, then state the winner and its final top-level BENCH_MS.

content.text:
Confirmed green: 0 test failures, checksum matches (1378644788), BENCH_MS 1735 at top level.

| Candidate | Tests | Checksum OK | BENCH_MS (3 runs) | Median |
|---|---|---|---|---|

Done. `./ledger.ts` now holds the winning candidate.

**Winner: caching repeated work — memoising `categoryLabel` and `buildFeeTable`. BENCH_MS 1735.**

| Strategy | Tests | Checksum | Median BENCH_MS |
|---|---|---|---|
| base (unmodified) | pass | ok | 3398 |
| memoise pure functions | pass | ok | **1723** |
| hoist loop-invariants | pass | ok | 2464 |
| hash set for watchlist | pass | ok | 2545 |
| single-pass duplicate table | pass | ok | 2643 |
| batch the I/O | pass | ok | 2678 |

All five candidates passed, and every one held `CHECKSUM: 1378644788` — so the pick was on speed alone. Memoisation won because `categoryLabel` and `buildFeeTable` were each being recomputed 16,000 times inside the transaction loop (`ledger.ts:148-149`), and both do thousands of `Math.imul` rounds per call.

One thing the harness structurally can't see: the five strategies are largely independent and would compose. Hoisting the fee table, the watchlist `Set`, and batched writes all attack different costs than memoisation does, so a combined version should beat 1735 comfortably. Say the word and I'll build and benchmark the merged candidate.
